parent_axis_record.name: star_sign
parent_axis_record.def: 1: textbook cancer (water sign — moody, sentimental, defensive, sensitive, nurturing, protective of inner circle) · 2: cancer-leaning · 3: hybrid / undefined · 4: sagittarian-leaning · 5: textbook sagittarius (fire sign — adventurous, blunt, freedom-seeking, philosophical, restless)

hypothesis.def1: 1: defensive and protective · 5: flirtatious and adventurous
hypothesis.def2: 1: microscopic and granular · 5: macroscopic and panoramic
hypothesis.rationale: The measurement shifted because 'Sagittarius' was being used to track social playfulness in one context and physical restlessness in another, while 'Cancer' was being used to track either emotional shielding or technical caution.

hypothesis.def1: 1: apprehensive and shielding · 5: blunt and expansionist
hypothesis.def2: 1: meticulous and detail-oriented · 5: restless and momentum-driven
hypothesis.rationale: The parent axis conflated 'Sagittarius' (freedom/bluntness) with 'action-oriented momentum', whereas the actual variance depends on whether the character is investigating via precision (low momentum) or physical exertion (high momentum).

hypothesis.def1: 1: cautious and protective · 5: impetuous and bold
hypothesis.def2: 1: precision-focused and sensory · 5: utility-focused and blunt
hypothesis.rationale: The variance suggests the parent axis was accidentally measuring 'action-readiness' (the degree of physical force/restlessness) independently from 'attention-type' (the degree of technical focus/nurturing of a task).
